channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: William E. Leuchtenburg, eminent presidential historian and Ken Burns consultant, dies at 102
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — William E. Leuchtenburg, a prize-winning historian widely admired for his authoritative writings on the U.S. presidency and as the reigning scholar on Franklin Roosevelt and the New Deal, has died at 102.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump is looking to boot transgender troops from the military. Here's why that's complicated
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has launched his second bid to oust all transgender troops from the military, and once again it will be headed to the courts to sort it out.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rihanna makes first court appearance at the trial of her partner A$AP Rocky, as accuser testifies
item.description: Rihanna appeared for the first time at the trial of her longtime partner A$AP Rocky, on the day of its most important testimony — the description by a former friend of the moment Rocky allegedly fired a gun at him.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Lawyers reach a deal on dividing a $4B settlement for Hawaii wildfire victims
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — A last-minute deal reached between lawyers representing victims of a deadly Hawaii wildfire has averted the need for a trial to determine how to split a $4 billion settlement. The unusual trial had been expected to delve into difficult questions about survivors’ losses.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House budget office rescinds federal funding freeze memo
item.description: The Trump’s administration’s Office of Management and Budget released a new memo Wednesday rescinding a controversial memo issued late Monday that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance, which had paralyzed many federal programs and caused a huge uproar on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Flawed emergency alert systems lagged when residents needed them most during Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When disaster strikes, government emergency alert systems offer a simple promise: Residents will get information about nearby dangers and instructions to help them stay safe.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, CLAUDIA LAUER, AMY TAXIN and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump fires two Democratic commissioners of agency that enforces civil rights laws in the workplace
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — President Donald Trump fired two of the three Democratic commissioners of the federal agency that enforces civil rights law in the workplace, an unprecedented move aimed at implementing his crackdown on certain diversity and gender rights policies.
item.dc:creator: ALEXANDRA OLSON and CLAIRE SAVAGE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump looks to repurpose federal money to expand school choice programs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Private school vouchers and other school choice initiatives would expand under an order coming from President Donald Trump telling government agencies to repurpose federal dollars.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Search underway for inmate who escaped via ventilation system: North Carolina sheriff
item.description: Authorities say the man's cellmates helped him escape and then stuffed a mattress to make it look like he was still sleeping.
item.dc:creator: Mekaela Muck, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Refugee resettlement agencies scramble after Trump orders them to halt their federally funded work
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Tens of thousands of recently arrived refugees might lose support for basic necessities like food and rent after a Trump administration order suspended federal funding for resettlement agencies.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY and PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump approval rating slips after flurry of executive actions: Survey
item.description: President Trump's approval rating slipped after his first week back in the Oval Office, where he issued a slew of executive orders.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Honda recalls more than 290,000 vehicles over possible engine issue
item.description: An error in the fuel injection electronic control unit could lead to the engine stalling or a loss os power.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Capitol Police arrest man with Molotov cocktails attempting to target Johnson, Hegseth, Bessent
item.description: The U.S. Capitol Police arrested a man with Molotov cocktails and knives who was allegedly attempting to kill House Speaker Mike Johnson and President Trump’s two recently confirmed nominees, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard involved in board meeting brawl between boyfriend, activist
item.description: Chicago-area "super mayor" Tiffany Henyard jumped into a Thornton Township board meeting fist fight between her boyfriend and an activist who criticized her.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Live updates: White House rescinds funding freeze, RFK Jr. faces tough questions
item.description: President Trump's executive actions, including a freeze on federal grants and a buyout offer to federal employees, have drawn criticism from Democrats and some Republicans, while his pick for HHS Secretary, Robert F. Kennedy, Jr.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, ABC 4

item.title: Frontier makes a second bid for Spirit with budget airlines struggling
item.description: Frontier Airlines is attempting for a second time to merge with the now bankrupt Spirit Airlines, which declared bankruptcy late last year as budget airlines struggle.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's Cabinet nominees up next for confirmation hearings and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top-tier college student, former high school football star killed in freak accident
item.description: Authorities confirmed that a Clemson University student died after he was crushed and trapped by a fallen tree in a freak accident over the weekend in South Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Miles-long cluster of dolphins captured on drone video
item.description: A miles-long cluster of dolphins has been filmed leaping and gliding across Carmel Bay off California, forming an unusual “super pod” of more than 1,500 of the marine creatures.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top NYPD officials faulted for 'demeaning,' 'unprofessional' social media posts attacking critics
item.description: Two NYPD chiefs violated department policy when they made "demeaning" social media posts to attack civil servants and journalists, an independent monitor found.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deeply disturbing statistics show Texas children under the age of 11 left state for abortion in 2023
item.description: Newly released statistics from 2023 show that minors in Texas are traveling out of the state to have abortions and the numbers are rising.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge has temporarily blocked a Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans that could total trillions of dollars. U.S. District Judge Loren L. AliKhan blocked the action Tuesday afternoon, minutes before it was set to go into effect.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Video: Truck smashes through front of Alabama gas station
item.description: Surveillance footage from the gas station shows a white truck smashing into the front of the store and hitting the front counter.
item.dc:creator: Richard Everett, NewsNation

item.title: Coca-Cola launches new Orange Cream flavor
item.description: A year after the launch of the short-lived Coca-Cola Spiced, Coke is adding another new flavor to its lineup.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Border agents shot at in South Texas
item.description: Border Patrol agents were fired upon Monday in the remote Starr County town of Fronton, Texas, Border Report has been told.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Additional Texas National Guard troops arrive at border
item.description: Over 400 Texas National Guard troops and equipment began arriving in the Rio Grande Valley on Monday evening to assist with border security.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Apple software update will activate AI by default, but there is a way to turn it off
item.description: iPhone users could have a somewhat surprising new feature on their devices after their next software update.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni get March 2026 trial date for her 'It Ends With Us' lawsuit
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York judge set a March 2026 trial date on Monday and moved an initial conference from mid-February to next week as the public feud between Blake Lively and her “It Ends With Us” costar and director Justin Baldoni continued to grow and accelerate.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Family, friends of Gabby Petito share details surrounding her death in upcoming Netflix documentary
item.description: An upcoming Netflix documentary will shed new light on the story of Gabby Petito and her death at the hands of her fiancé, Brian Laundrie.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WGN 9

item.title: Full-scale replica of Anne Frank's hidden annex opens in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A full-scale replica of the secret annex where Anne Frank penned her famous diary opened in New York City on Monday as the world marked International Holocaust Remembrance Day.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO and JOSEPH FREDERICK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prosecutors say woman charged in killing of Border Patrol agent was in contact with homicide suspect
item.description: A Washington state woman charged in connection with the fatal shooting of a U.S. border patrol agent in Vermont had been in frequent contact with someone whom authorities have linked to homicides in Pennsylvania and California, a federal prosecutor said in court documents Monday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, KATHY McCORMACK and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3.8 magnitude earthquake in Maine felt in Massachusetts, Rhode Island
item.description: A Ware, Massachusetts, resident said she was home and her whole house was shaking for about 10 seconds from what she at first believed was an 18-wheeler coming down Route 9.
item.dc:creator: Emma McCorkindale, Fox 5

item.title: Beneficiaries under Social Security Fairness Act could be waiting 'more than one year' for adjusted payments, SSA says
item.description: The Social Security Administration, in an update to its website on Friday, said the department does not currently have the funding to execute the adjustments “in a timely manner and without negatively affecting day-to-day customer service.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Is cell service getting worse? One study suggests that it is
item.description: The study, from J.D. Power, showed an increase in reported wireless network problems between the first and the second half of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, ABC 4

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gabbard's path through Intelligence Committee narrows ahead of key hearing
item.description: Republicans are cautioning that Tulsi Gabbard’s path to confirmation to lead the U.S. intelligence apparatus is narrowing as she seemingly has trouble winning over key GOP senators. Gabbard, along with Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Top Trump administration officials in Chicago for start of immigration enforcement crackdown
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Top Trump administration officials, including “border czar” Tom Homan and the acting deputy attorney general, visited Chicago on Sunday to witness the start of ramped-up immigration enforcementin the nation’s third-largest city as federal agencies touted arrests around the country.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4
